perturbation.original_text: 2.The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Information for [five (5)] years from the date of disclosure, and shall not disclose the Information to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Information for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.changed_text: 2.The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Information and shall not disclose the Information to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Information for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'for [five (5)] years from the date of disclosure' from clause 2, the duration that the receiving party is required to maintain confidentiality of the information is now ambiguous, creating an in-text contradiction with the obligations in Clause 3. Clause 3 details what needs to happen 'forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination', but there is no longer a clearly defined period of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it takes in protecting its own confidential information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a five-year confidentiality period. The changed text removes this duration, making the confidentiality period undefined. The contradicted text in clause (c) details actions required 'forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination', which implies a finite confidentiality period. Without a defined duration, the trigger for these actions becomes ambiguous, creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it takes in protecting its own confidential information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it takes in protecting its own confidential information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only;
perturbation.explanation: By removing subclause (c) from clause 3, which outlines the actions to be taken upon a written request or termination, it contradicts the assumed obligation to return or destroy confidential information at some point. Since there is no clear end date to the confidentiality (see previous perturbations), failing to specify return or destruction mechanisms means the receiving party could, theoretically, hold the information indefinitely. This omission contradicts the intent of the NDA to protect information only for a defined and reasonable timeframe implied from other sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Information and shall not disclose the Information to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Information for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.justification: The original text's clause 3(c) explicitly details the actions the Receiving Party must take upon request or termination of the agreement, including returning or destroying confidential information. The changed text omits this entire subclause. The contradicted text states the Receiving Party's obligation to keep the Information confidential and only use it for the Authorised Purpose. By removing the requirement to return or destroy the information as detailed in original text's clause 3(c), the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, because without a return or destruction obligation, the Receiving Party could theoretically hold confidential information indefinitely, not just for the authorized purpose, and thus, the confidentiality clause is weakened. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as the changed text no longer provides a mechanism to enforce the limited use and eventual return/destruction of confidential information, thus creating an in-text contradiction per the 'Omissions' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information,' the altered clause now lacks a crucial disclaimer of liability. Removing this sentence implies that the Disclosing Party may, in fact, be held liable for losses or damages that arise from the Receiving Party's use of confidential information even though the agreement is an 'as is' agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction as it becomes uncertain what is the liability between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a disclaimer of liability for the Disclosing Party regarding the Receiving Party's use of information. The changed text omits this disclaimer. The contradicted text is effectively the same as the original text; since the changed text removes the disclaimer that was originally present in both the original and contradicted text, the Disclosing Party could now be liable for losses or damages resulting from the Receiving Party's use of the information. This creates an in-text contradiction because one version explicitly disclaims liability while the other, by omission, implies potential liability within the same agreement regarding the same subject matter (use of information). This fits the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition because essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding relative to the protection of the Information covered by this Agreement, is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'This Agreement contains the entire understanding relative to the protection of the Information covered by this Agreement', the contract no longer explicitly claims to be the complete agreement on protecting the information. This absence creates a potential contradiction and ambiguity regarding whether other agreements or understandings might exist concerning the information. It undermines the 'entire agreement' clause and could allow parties to argue that other undocumented agreements exist. It contradicts the purpose of the agreement itself and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an entire agreement clause, stating that the agreement contains the entire understanding regarding the protection of information. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The removal of the entire agreement clause directly contradicts the implicit assumption within the contract that it is the sole agreement governing the protection of information and creates ambiguity, meaning other agreements related to the information could exist which creates a strong contradiction in text. According to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', removing essential language that creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections triggers a contradiction.
